title: Two required algorithm boxes must avoid any O(M)/O(N) object

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G6 (Lemma L6).** The problem explicitly requires an algorithm box for BOTH (i) the direct symmetric method and (ii) the PCG iterative method.

Constraints both boxes must satisfy: never instantiate $Z\otimes K$ ($nr\times nr$), the dense $T$ ($n\times M$), or anything indexed by $M$ or $N$.

- **Direct symmetric box:** assemble $\mathcal A$ via the observed contraction $\sum_\ell(\tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^T)\otimes(\tilde K_\ell\tilde K_\ell^T)$ (assembly $O(qn^2r^2)$) then symmetric (Cholesky) solve $O(n^3r^3)$ — assembly must itself avoid $O(M),O(N)$.
- **PCG box:** standard preconditioned CG using the implicit matvec (Gap G1) and the $O(M)$-free preconditioner (Gap G2), with stopping criterion and iteration bound.

**Required:** both boxes, line-by-line consistent with the lemmas, with per-line cost annotations confirming no $O(M),O(N)$ work.
